title: ⭐ Practice: Identifying Discourse Markers & Their Functions

options.text: Contrast — it introduces an opposing idea.
Addition — it adds a supporting or stronger point.
Result — it signals a consequence.
Concession — it acknowledges a counterpoint.

questions.explanation: "Moreover" signals addition — it introduces a further point that supports or strengthens what was just said: The plan is affordable; moreover, it is efficient. It is more formal than "also" or "and". It does not introduce contrast (however/whereas), result (therefore/thus), or concession (nevertheless/albeit).

questions.prompt: Complete: "She studied for months. ___, she passed the exam with distinction."

options.text: However
Nevertheless
Consequently
Whereas

questions.explanation: Studying for months caused her to pass — a logical result: Consequently, she passed. "However" and "Nevertheless" signal contrast/concession — a contradiction or override. "Whereas" introduces contrast between two different things in a single sentence — not appropriate for two separate sentences expressing a cause-effect chain.

options.text: He preferred tea. Whereas she preferred coffee.
He preferred tea, whereas she preferred coffee.
Whereas. He preferred tea; she preferred coffee.
He preferred tea whereas. She preferred coffee.

questions.explanation: "Whereas" is a subordinating conjunction — it must join two clauses IN ONE SENTENCE: He preferred tea, whereas she preferred coffee. It cannot begin a new sentence on its own (a), cannot stand alone with a full stop after it (c), and cannot appear at the end of a clause followed by a full stop (d).

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ in spite of their fears, the volunteers entered the building."

options.text: However
Despite
Although
(no connector needed — 'in spite of' is the connector)

questions.explanation: "In spite of" is already a prepositional phrase acting as the concessive connector: In spite of their fears, the volunteers entered the building. No additional connector is needed before it. Adding "However", "Despite" (redundant), or "Although" (which needs a full clause) would create a double connector and a grammatical error.

questions.prompt: What does 'albeit' mean in: "The policy was effective, albeit controversial"?

options.text: As a result of being controversial
Even though it was controversial
Which means it was controversial
And furthermore it was controversial

questions.explanation: "Albeit" means "even though" or "although" — it introduces a concessive qualification: effective, even though controversial. It acknowledges the drawback while the main clause (effective) stands. Option (a) would be a result (because of). Option (c) would be a clarification (namely/that is). Option (d) would be an addition (moreover).

questions.prompt: Find the INCORRECT use of a discourse marker.

options.text: The team worked efficiently; hence, the project was completed early.
The results were mixed; nevertheless, the board approved the proposal.
He improved the software, thereby increasing user satisfaction.
She failed the test. Furthermore, she didn't study enough.

questions.explanation: Option (d) has the wrong marker: "Furthermore" adds a supporting point — but "she didn't study enough" explains WHY she failed; it is a cause/reason, not an additional point. A better marker would be "Besides" or a causal connector like "She failed the test, perhaps because she hadn't studied enough." Options (a), (b), and (c) correctly use hence (result), nevertheless (concession), and thereby + -ing (result embedded in clause).
